evals.name: session-start-query
evals.prompt: I'm about to work on the authentication module in our Django project. Before I start, check if there's any relevant context from previous sessions.
evals.expected_output: Agent queries bkmr with hsearch for auth/Django memories filtered by _mem_ tag, checks for gotchas, and presents findings. Does NOT attempt to store anything.

assertions.text: Uses bkmr hsearch or bkmr search with -t _mem_ flag
Includes --json and --np flags for machine-readable non-interactive output
Queries for gotchas or preferences in addition to general topic search
Does NOT attempt to store/add any new memory

evals.name: post-task-memory-write
evals.prompt: We just discovered that the CI pipeline fails silently when the Docker registry is unreachable — it reports success but the image never pushes. The fix was adding --push-verify to the docker build step in the Makefile.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this as a gotcha worth storing. Runs dedup check via hsearch first. Then stores with 'bkmr add' using -t mem, gotcha classification tag, descriptive title, and --no-web. Content is a concise summary, not full Makefile contents.

assertions.text: Performs dedup check before storing (hsearch or search with _mem_ tag)
Includes --no-web flag on the add command
Content is a concise summary under 1000 tokens, not a full file dump

evals.name: dedup-scenario
evals.prompt: Remember that our staging database is PostgreSQL 15 on port 5433.
evals.expected_output: Agent searches for existing memories about staging database. If a match exists, updates/edits the existing entry rather than creating a duplicate. If no match, creates a new fact memory with appropriate tags.

assertions.text: Searches existing memories before attempting to write (dedup check)
Uses _mem_ tag filter in the dedup search
If match found: uses bkmr edit or bkmr update instead of bkmr add
If no match: creates new memory with 'fact' classification tag
Does NOT blindly create a new memory without checking first
